question: Based on the chart, what can be concluded about performance when experience reaches 150 years?

options.A: Performance will definitely continue to decrease as experience increases to 150 years.
options.B: The performance score at 150 years of experience can be reliably read from the chart.
options.C: Cannot be determined due to data variability and because 150 years is beyond the experience levels shown.
options.D: Performance will definitely improve again once experience exceeds 120 years.

explanation: While the trend line suggests decreasing performance, the data shows significant variability and only goes up to 100 years. Extrapolating to 150 years involves uncertainty, and option C implies a level of certainty that cannot be guaranteed given the scatter in the data. The concealed uncertainty lies in the variability around the trend line and the act of extrapolation beyond the observed data range.
